questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Let $\left(1+x+x^2\right)^{20}=a_0+a_1 x+a_2 x^2+\ldots \ldots+a_{40} x^{40}$. Further $S=\sum_{r=0}^{40} a_r$. Two coefficients are chosen from the coefficients $\mathrm{a}_0, \mathrm{a}_1, \ldots, \mathrm{a}_{40}$ and the probability that they are equal is p (considering no three coefficients are equal). Units digit of $S$ is equal to $b$ and $a=\frac{1-p}{10 p}$. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question If $(\sqrt{a-b}+b)^6=I+F$, where $0 \leq F \lt 1$ and $I \in N$, then the value of $I$ is -
questions.solution:
$S=3^{20}$
unit's digit of $S$ is $b=1$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Also } \mathrm{p}=\frac{{ }^{20} \mathrm{C}_1}{{ }^{41} \mathrm{C}_2}=\frac{1}{41} \\
& \therefore \quad \mathrm{a}=4
\end{aligned}$

$(\sqrt{3}+1)^6=\mathrm{I}+\mathrm{F} \text { where } 0 \leq \mathrm{F} \lt 1$
Let $(\sqrt{3}-1)^6=\mathrm{G}$ where $0 \leq \mathrm{G} \lt 1$
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{I}+\mathrm{F}+\mathrm{G}=(\sqrt{3}+1)^6+(\sqrt{3}-1)^6$
$=2\left\{{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_0(\sqrt{3})^6+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_2(\sqrt{3})^4+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_4(\sqrt{3})^2+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_6\right\}$
$=2\{1.27+15.9+15.3+1\}=416$
But $0 \leq \mathrm{F}+\mathrm{G} \lt 2$ and $\mathrm{F}+\mathrm{G}$ has to be an integer
$\therefore \quad I=416-1=415$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Let $\left(1+x+x^2\right)^{20}=a_0+a_1 x+a_2 x^2+\ldots \ldots+a_{40} x^{40}$. Further $S=\sum_{r=0}^{40} a_r$. Two coefficients are chosen from the coefficients $\mathrm{a}_0, \mathrm{a}_1, \ldots, \mathrm{a}_{40}$ and the probability that they are equal is p (considering no three coefficients are equal). Units digit of $S$ is equal to $b$ and $a=\frac{1-p}{10 p}$. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question The coefficient $\mathrm{a}_3$ is equal to -
questions.solution:
$S=3^{20}$
unit's digit of $S$ is $b=1$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Also } \mathrm{p}=\frac{{ }^{20} \mathrm{C}_1}{{ }^{41} \mathrm{C}_2}=\frac{1}{41} \\
& \therefore \quad \mathrm{a}=4
\end{aligned}$

The general term in the expansion of $\left(1+x+x^2\right)^{20}$ is $\frac{20!}{r!s!t!} 1^r x^s x^{2 t}$
where $r+s+t=20$ for coefficient of $x^3, s+2 t=3$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad t=0, s=3, r=17 \\ & \text { or } t=1, s=1, r=18\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad$ coefficient of $\mathrm{x}^3=\frac{20!}{17!3!}+\frac{20!}{18!1!}=\frac{4.20!}{18!}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
